title: LEAVE Japan
wikitext:
[]: LEAVE Japan (spoilers aha)             Quote Post by wonderluster » Sat Sep 12, 2009 7:54 pm Just why the HELL didn't the Japanese get moved out by force? Seriously, the first thing the authorities should do if your city is about to become a warzone against the most powerful not to mention supernatural beings on the planet is GET CIVILIANS OUT. When Zeruel came in Rebuild, once he broke through into the Geofront, does anybody remember how many of the shelters were falling? That's worse than any act of terrorism and could have EASILY BEEN PREVENTED. Big fail Seele. Big fail Nerv. I don't even like Kensuke.

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Sat Sep 12, 2009 8:03 pm Might want to change the topic title.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 12, 2009 8:15 pm This applies to NGE as well. If you're going to store something (i.e. Lilith) that monsters are all trying to reach, store it away from a populated area.

[]:             Quote Post by CATO » Sat Sep 12, 2009 8:29 pm I disagree with you. First. Zeruel didn't give them any time to evacuate. Second. Nerv HQ need a working force. Who do you think are the ones cleaning the thousands of floors they must have? Yes, civilians! They need secretaries, office boys, doctors, researchers, undergrads doing fellowships, PR people, guards, technicians, assistants, cooks for the cafeteria and lots, let me repeat, LOTS of psychiatrists for their VIP people (aka pilots and military personnel). Besides the fact that Tokyo 3 is the perfect cover up for Nerv, well at least before Sachiel showed up.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sat Sep 12, 2009 8:33 pm Yeah. Those psycharitrists are impotant. Look at the miraculous work they did with the piliots.

[]:             Quote Post by CATO » Sat Sep 12, 2009 8:35 pm Yeah. Those psycharitrists are impotant. Look at the miraculous work they did with the piliots.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Sat Sep 12, 2009 8:35 pm They didn't leave Japan because everywhere else just sucks.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sun Sep 13, 2009 7:16 am Well, fanon is that pretty much the only people still in T3 after Sachiel were NERV employees, their families, and just enough people to run all the required facilities (power, food, waterworks, and other utilities), hence seemingly only the one room in Misato's building being occupied etc

[]:             Quote Post by R-U-4-Zeruel » Sun Sep 13, 2009 6:26 pm Well CATO did say Zeruel suprised them and they did evacuate the whole of tokyo when Sahaquiel made an appearance. So far none of the angels (NGE speaking) have hurt any humans until Zeruel stormed the gates of tokyo and I'm betting that in rebuild 1.0 and 2.0 only nerv employed personel or soldiers have killed - Ramiel blasting eva unit 01 when it sniped her metalic shiny ass and clockiel when it performed its first on screen attack on those navy ships - just my opinion, Hi by the way

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Sep 14, 2009 3:28 am R-U-4-Zeruel wrote: Well CATO did say Zeruel suprised them and they did evacuate the whole of tokyo when Sahaquiel made an appearance. So far none of the angels (NGE speaking) have hurt any humans

[]:             Quote Post by R-U-4-Zeruel » Mon Sep 14, 2009 3:40 am urgh how on earth could I forget Toji's sister*facepalm*, but what I was getting at was that the bunkers have worked pretty damn well so far, both in NGE and RoE, well up until Zeruel's chance at instrumantality. He and ramiel were the only angels that made it into the geofront and zeruel compared to ramiel done far more excessive damage. What good is a city without it's people ;) or penguins

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Sep 15, 2009 2:52 pm 1. they didn't just store lilith under tokyo-3, they built tokyo-3 on top of lilith. 2. i actually rather applaud their evacuation protocols-- they always seemed rather efficient to me, for some reason. it's just that some people were idiots who didn't care for staying safe (like touji and kensuke), and those who did, well, you can only build a shelter so strong on a limited budget. 3. Japan is somehow the heart of the universe. if you don't agree, just watch more anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Tue Sep 15, 2009 4:28 pm Heh. yeah, it was I think Anime Turnpike or something like that that had the laws of anime or something listed on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Sep 15, 2009 6:08 pm They built tokyo 3 on top of Lilith because that's where the black moon was, there's probably no way to move that.

[]:             Quote Post by R-U-4-Zeruel » Tue Sep 15, 2009 6:30 pm Ornette wrote: They built tokyo 3 on top of Lilith because that's where the black moon was, there's probably no way to move that.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Tue Sep 15, 2009 7:44 pm The Black Moon is HUGE! If the shots from EOE IIRC seem to indicate it's diameter is bigger than Japan, and completely embedded in the Earth's crust. I doubt you can just pick it up and move it

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Tue Sep 15, 2009 7:47 pm If Lilith's room is in the center, what's at the very bottom of the Black Moon?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Sep 15, 2009 9:06 pm Who said her room was in the center? But seeing as the Black Moon was essentially a spaceship, it'd need a lot of room for reinforced walls and such, and there's probably shit for holding LCL and souls and all that.
